title: Pharmaceuticals
subtitle: Active ingredients through manufacturing, distribution, and insurance to the patient, and the handful of firms the whole system cannot route around.
financials_note: Five-year income-statement trends for the chokepoints. Revenue bars read on the left; margins read on the right. Most chokepoints here are SEC filers or major public companies on developed exchanges, so expect solid source tiers on accounting data.
chokepoints_note: The companies the rest of the chain has the fewest alternatives to. Drug originators and generic makers are comparatively diverse; the API layer, sterile manufacturing, physical distribution, and insurance administration are where routing options run out. Every call here is a foundation hypothesis until relationship edges test it.

layers.label: API producers
layers.desc: Manufacture active pharmaceutical ingredients and chemical precursors

layers.label: Drug originators
layers.desc: Develop and market branded drugs across therapeutic areas

layers.label: Generic manufacturers
layers.desc: Produce generic versions of off-patent drugs

layers.label: Contract manufacturers
layers.desc: Provide sterile fill-finish, biologics, and small-molecule manufacturing for originators and generics

layers.label: Distributors
layers.desc: Move finished pharmaceuticals from makers to pharmacies and hospitals

layers.label: Pharmacies
layers.desc: Dispense drugs to patients at retail and specialty locations

layers.label: Pharmacy benefit managers
layers.desc: Administer insurance claims and manage formulary access and rebates

layers.label: Payers
layers.desc: Provide health insurance and fund drug purchases

chokepoint_why.Zhejiang Huahai Pharmaceutical: A large share of the world's generic drug active pharmaceutical ingredients come from a small number of Chinese and Indian plants; Huahai's 2018 valsartan/sartan API contamination triggered a global multi-country recall, showing how concentrated this layer is and how a single plant failure can halt production worldwide.
chokepoint_why.Scientific Protein Laboratories: Supplies nearly all crude heparin, an anticoagulant extracted from pig intestine with no synthetic substitute at scale, from a sourcing chain in Changzhou China; the 2007-2008 contaminated-heparin crisis traced to SPL's supply chain killed patients in the US.
chokepoint_why.Lonza Group: A handful of contract development and manufacturing organizations provide the sterile fill-finish and biologics capacity that originators increasingly depend on; Lonza is one of the few with large-scale biologics and sterile-injectable lines, and a shutdown here cannot be quickly absorbed elsewhere.
chokepoint_why.WuXi Biologics: One of the world's largest biologics CDMOs; capacity is genuinely scarce because specialized cleanrooms and bioreactor trains take years to build, and WuXi is explicitly named in the proposed US BIOSECURE Act, creating a geopolitical as well as industrial chokepoint.
chokepoint_why.McKesson Corporation: One of the big-three US drug wholesalers; McKesson, Cencora, and Cardinal Health together move roughly 90 percent of US pharmaceutical volume, and almost every manufacturer routes through one of these three with no fourth-scale alternative.
chokepoint_why.CVS Health (Caremark): CVS Caremark, Express Scripts, and OptumRx together administer roughly 80 percent of US prescription claims; PBMs decide formulary placement and negotiate rebates that determine a drug's net price, giving this layer outsized leverage over both manufacturers and patients.
